steps.title: Use the essential name
steps.content: The sentence introduces the specific wildlife biologist who is on the front lines of conservation efforts. “Dr. Purnima Devi Barman” is essential because it identifies which wildlife biologist the sentence means, so no punctuation should separate the title and name from “biologist.”

steps.title: Eliminate unnecessary punctuation
steps.content: Choice A sets the name off with commas, incorrectly treating it as nonessential. Choice B leaves an opening comma before the name without a closing comma and still separates essential information. Choice C uses a colon where direct identification is needed. The unpunctuated phrase correctly identifies the biologist. The answer is D.
